description_long: This guard rail consists of a horizontal top rail supported by four evenly spaced vertical posts, all constructed from what appears to be metal tubing. The design is simple and utilitarian, with clean, straight lines and a uniform dark gray finish. The rail is likely intended for use as a pedestrian or light vehicle barrier in urban environments such as sidewalks, parks, or parking lots. The ends of the rail are blunt and squared off, with no visible flaring or additional safety features. The surface appears smooth and hard, with a matte to slightly glossy finish that suggests powder-coated or painted steel. There are no visible reflective markings or decorative elements, emphasizing its functional purpose.
description: A sturdy, dark gray metal guard rail with four vertical posts and a smooth, utilitarian finish. Designed for urban safety barriers, it features a simple, functional structure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width, four vertical posts, and the horizontal top rail.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile and depth of the rail, with posts aligned in a single plane.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the same four vertical posts and top rail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and depth of the structure.
